steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
The left side forms an incomplete independent clause: “What increases is the rate at which the water turns to” (awaiting an object).
The right side contains a second independent clause: “an airtight seal traps the vapor in the pot, creating pressure...” (with “a pressure cooker pot” as an introductory noun phrase).

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
Two independent clauses must be joined with a period, semicolon, or comma plus a coordinating conjunction.

Choice A (‘vapor. With’) uses a period, which correctly separates the two independent clauses into distinct sentences.
The other options fail this rule:
  • C creates a comma splice (joining with a comma but no coordinating conjunction)
  • D creates a run-on (no punctuation)
  • B creates an ungrammatical phrase

That confirms A is correct.
